examples: Unlike the general run of peasantry they had a taste for flamboyant dress and gesture; but they usually shared the peasants' religious beliefs and superstitions.
But they generally engage in Labour themselves and in this sense are part of the peasantry.
To some extent these forces emanate from the peasantry, whose ties with the land are under threat.
He left the factory world at 22, changed by the experience of grassroots peasantry and poverty.
An American had never had such access to the deepest thoughts of the Chinese peasantry.
